thinkpool
Product Hunt launch · decision gate 1

Six evidence-led directions for the gallery’s first conversation.

Each direction is a rough framing test, built around one unedited live room capture. The choice is positioning, not a final launch asset.

thinkpool / product hunt gallery
Direction A · 01 / 06

The coding agents multiplied. The human stayed alone.

Thinkpool gives the work a second pair of eyes.

For technical cofounders and trusted engineering pairs. Do not supervise hard agent work alone.

PAIRTwo names. One room. Either can redirect the build.
Rough direction · live product capture · 14 Jul 2026Human presence is the proof
Direction A / another human in the loop
Support · 02 / 06

Two names, one decision surface.

The room records a partner’s concern, a reply, and the shared handoff in the same visible thread.

Evidence in frame
Conrad names the drift.
Maks directs the change.
@pool is the visible handoff.
01Conrad raises the concern
02Maks redirects the work
03@pool makes the shared direction explicit
Rough direction · live product capture · 14 Jul 2026Names, not anonymous operators
thinkpool / product hunt gallery
Direction B · 03 / 06

Fan-out is not teamwork.

Every delegated lane stays visible to both people in the room.

For agent power users drowning in tabs. Visibility gives the pair a way to stay in control.

VISIBLEThe work moves across lanes without disappearing from the room.
Rough direction · live product capture · 14 Jul 2026Visible work, not a hidden tree
Direction B / no hidden agent tree
Support · 04 / 06

The work stays in view.

Tabs, proof review, dispatch, and tool evidence are all present in the same room capture. No invented command center required.

Evidence in frame
Terminal tabs
D1 proof review
Pool dispatch
Tool activity
01Terminal tabs are visible
02D1 proof review has its own lane
03Dispatch and tool work remain inspectable
Rough direction · live product capture · 14 Jul 2026Specific visibility, not a uniqueness claim
thinkpool / product hunt gallery
Direction C · 05 / 06 · recommended

The agent gets one direction. You keep the debate.

Talk in the room. Keep an aside between the pair. Use @pool when the shared instruction is ready.

For cofounder and product-engineer pairs. Reach a shared direction before the agent builds.

RECOMMENDEDFrom room decision to working agent.
Rough direction · live product capture · 14 Jul 2026Narrowest differentiated mechanism
Direction C / agree, then build
Support · 06 / 06 · recommended

From room decision to working agent.

The pictured evidence is the on-record discussion, visible @pool handoff, and agent work. The private aside is a product mechanic, not fabricated screenshot content.

Three visible steps
01 Pair discussion
02 @pool handoff
03 Agent work + review
01On-record pair discussion
02@pool is the visible shared handoff
03Agent work and review stay visible
Rough direction · live product capture · 14 Jul 2026Visible proof only
